medications.indication_rationale: Appropriate for suspected ACS but dangerous in suspected aortic dissection due to bleeding risk into the false lumen or pericardium; must not be self-administered given the atypical tearing, jaw-and-back-radiating pain with syncope.

medications.drug_name: any self-directed blood pressure lowering medication (e.g. extra dose of usual antihypertensive)
medications.indication_rationale: Blood pressure control in dissection must be titrated IV by paramedics/ED staff; uncontrolled self-dosing risks inadequate or excessive effect.
contraindications_in_this_case: Suspected aortic dissection pending paramedic assessment

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Remain completely still, ideally lying down, and avoid any exertion or movement until paramedics arrive.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Movement increases shear stress on the aortic wall, risking extension or rupture of the dissection.

patient_education_points.point: Your symptoms — sudden tearing chest pain going to your jaw, with a near-fainting episode and a history of high blood pressure — raise concern for a tear in the main artery from your heart (aortic dissection), a life-threatening emergency.
Call 000 for an ambulance right now if you haven't already.
Do not take aspirin — unlike a heart attack, aspirin could be harmful if this is a tear in the aorta.
Stay as still as possible, lying down, until the ambulance arrives — try not to move around.

follow_up_plan.interval: Immediate emergency transport and hospital admission for emergency cardiothoracic surgical evaluation
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: CT aortogram findings, need for emergency surgery, blood pressure control targets, and long-term hypertension management
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Not applicable — already an emergency requiring immediate transport

must_include_items: Explicit instruction NOT to take aspirin
Instruction to remain completely still/avoid exertion
Recognition of aortic dissection as distinct from simple ACS
errors_of_commission: Recommending aspirin as for a standard heart attack
Suggesting the patient walk or move around
Recommending self-directed blood pressure medication dosing
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Recommending aspirin in this context is a critical, potentially fatal safety failure and should result in automatic case failure.
